source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Nisa has 3 different types of cards in a game: apple <image1>, cherry <image2> and grapes <image3>. She chooses 2 cards from the set and swaps their places. She wants to arrange the cards so that all the cards with the same fruit on are next to each other. For which set is this NOT possible?
<image4>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels; actual option content appears to be in the image
answer is a label only because no option text is provided outside the image

image_dependency: choices only provide labels A-E, so the actual sets to inspect are not available in text
question asks 'For which set', implying visual comparison of candidate sets shown in the image
multi_step: task requires checking whether a swap can make same-fruit cards contiguous
question asks which set is NOT possible, implying comparison across multiple candidate sets
reasoning likely combines visual identification of card order with swap feasibility
there are 5 answer choices, suggesting evaluation of multiple cases
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single labeled answer 'A'
question has a determinate multiple-choice format
once the image sets are read, each option can be checked by explicit swap feasibility

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no reported issues
core assets are present: question text, one existing primary image, and a valid multiple-choice answer
although the options are image-dependent and not described in text, the required visual content is available, so the core semantics remain recoverable
initial scoring indicates strong image dependency, solid multi-step reasoning, and good verifiability
this fits a high-value visual reasoning sample rather than an asset-deficient one
